rag_rules: Preserve existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections
Use assign only for existing output features or declared auxiliary variables in implementation contracts
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type scopes when concrete bounds or visible relations are available
generic_rules: Maintain component type and implementation scope separation
Ensure all referenced features are visible in the target scope
Avoid using AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions without explicit ports or constants
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations
Use nested if ... then ... else for branching logic
Use AGREE logical operators: and, or, not(...), and implication =>
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit ports or constants
irrelevant_topics: AGREE assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports should be placed in valid component type scopes
Implementation-level bindings should be placed in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
Subcomponent ports should not be used in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs
